17.dv: rarely (shortened) גֹּי; apparently from the same root as H1465 (גֵּוָה) (in the sense of massing);

17.gl: the arm (as stretched out), or (of animals) the foreleg; figuratively, force
17.kj: arm, [phrase] help, mighty, power, shoulder, strength.
17.dv: or (shortened) זְרֹעַ; and (feminine) זְרוֹעָה; or זְרֹעָה; from H2232 (זָרַע);

17.gl: drought; also a cutting instrument (from its destructive effect), as a knife, sword, or other sharp implement
17.kj: axe, dagger, knife, mattock, sword, tool.

18.gl: to descend (literally, to go downwards; or conventionally to a lower region, as the shore, a boundary, the enemy, etc.; or figuratively, to fall); causatively, to bring down (in all the above applications)
18.kj: [idiom] abundantly, bring down, carry down, cast down, (cause to) come(-ing) down, fall (down), get down, go(-ing) down(-ward), hang down, [idiom] indeed, let down, light (down), p

18.gl: who? (occasionally, by a peculiar idiom, of things); also (indefinitely) whoever; often used in oblique construction with prefix or suffix
18.kj: any (man), [idiom] he, [idiom] him, [phrase] O that! what, which, who(-m, -se, -soever), [phrase] would to God.
18.dv: an interrogative pronoun of persons, as H4100 (מָה) is of things,

18.gl: a bisection, i.e. (by implication) the centre
18.kj: among(-st), [idiom] between, half, [idiom] (there-, where-), in(-to), middle, mid(-night), midst (among), [idiom] out (of), [idiom] through, [idiom] with(-in).
18.dv: from an unused root meaning to sever;
a variation of H3068 (יְהֹוָה) (used after H136 (אֲדֹנָי), and pronounced by Jews as H430 (אֱלֹהִים), in order to prevent the repetition of the same s

18.gl: to lie down (for rest, sexual connection, decease or any other purpose)
18.kj: [idiom] at all, cast down, (lover-)lay (self) (down), (make to) lie (down, down to sleep, still with), lodge, ravish, take rest, sleep, stay.

18.gl: the earth (at large, or partitively a land)
18.kj: [idiom] common, country, earth, field, ground, land, [idiom] natins, way, [phrase] wilderness, world.
18.dv: from an unused root probably meaning to be firm;
